input.background:
Claim amounts for wind damage to insured homes are independent random variables with a common density function:

$$
f(x)= \begin{cases}3 x^{-4} & \text { for } x>1 \\ 0 & \text { otherwise }\end{cases}
$$

where $x$ is the amount of a claim in thousands. Suppose 3 such claims will be made.
input.question: What is the expected value of the largest of the three claims?

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 1: Basic Probability, Statistics, and Reliability
